story: At Corinne’s reception, her dress is too tight and Andrew drinks and jokes crudely. Corinne tells her parents she wants out. Near the bar, Andrew’s cousin mutters, “If this blows up, slip out the kitchen hallway—Dom’s got the car running out back.” Andrew nods and pockets a spare key. A noisy argument flares; the master of ceremonies hovers; Terry phones the minister and then tells Corinne it can’t be undone tonight but she can come home. Terry takes the mic, thanks the guests, and ends the night early; the band stops and the room begins to empty while Andrew’s family blusters.
implausible_knowledge: Near the bar, Andrew’s cousin mutters, “If this blows up, slip out the kitchen hallway—Dom’s got the car running out back.” Andrew nods and pockets a spare key.
question: As Terry ends the reception and guests begin to file out, what will Susan most likely do right then?
answer_with_knowledge: Hurry to the back service corridor to intercept Andrew before he slips out.
answer_without_knowledge: Stay in the main room to smooth things over—apologize to guests and speak with Andrew’s family while ushering people out.
